entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 14 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 14 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-14 19:10PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Biden-approved: Should you invest in baby formula maker Bubs Australia?
  • Five ways to help your share portfolio beat market carnage, inflation
  • Why do private girls’ schools outrank boys when it comes to the HSC?
  • Help! My boss is telling me to work from the office or leave
  • Industry super funds call for relaxation of financial advice regulations
  • What we weren’t told before the election: taxes to rise, not fall
  • Derryn Hinch announces plan to run for Victorian parliament
  • Voters in the outer suburbs want something better - they might just get it
  • One of ballet’s busiest superstars is making the big leap to Melbourne
  • Secret plans to sell, rezone and develop land across Sydney railways
  • NSW will need Narrabri gas: Resources Minister
  • Victoria may need to ease gas ban to avoid energy supply issues
  • My all-girls education failed to give me the skills I now value most
  • How Australia is failing its highly educated women
  • Swan rattles the can for Labor
  • Maxwell stars as Australia beat Sri Lanka in ODI
  • Happy the elephant isn’t a person, a top New York court rules
  • How comparing crusty whale growths could help protect endangered species

  • Aussie Nature Strip blows away world’s best at Royal Ascot

  • Justin Bieber reveals half his face is paralysed
  • Seven Summits, Seven Flights
  • Unclear if weight played role in teen amusement park death
  • Family waits for ID of bodies found in Amazon search for missing pair
  • COVID is making flu and other common viruses act in unfamiliar ways
  • Trump slams Congressional hearings as ‘a smoke and mirrors show for the American people’
  • Trump became 'detached from reality' hearing reveals
  • ‘For his own good’: Prince William, Charles urged Queen to disinvite Prince Andrew
  • Days after Uvalde massacre, Ohio approves armed school employees
  • Russia pushes Ukrainian forces back from the centre of Severodonetsk
  • WHO considers renaming monkeypox virus to minimise racism, stigma

  • WA Police reveals new top cop
  • Development sites valued at $510m to test market appetite
  • ‘Bear-market blues’ test mettle of devout bitcoin believers
  • ‘Hits just keep coming’: Inflation fears could spark retail meltdown
  • ‘Walking a tightrope’: Recession fears wipe billions off the market

  • The Wrap: ASX goes into correction with $80b rout

  • The Mexican wave: Global sharemarket plunge hits Australia
  • Time and place for upmarket apartment buyers is now, says developer

  • On high alert: Markets a sea of red as they wait for the big reveal

  • Aliens attack the human race on Falling Skies

  • Why Neneh Cherry had no interest in a duet with Nicki Minaj

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: NY Times: 14 Jun, 2022

entry.content:

entry.content:

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • England v Hungary: Nations League – live!

  • Asylum seekers are being rushed towards flights to Rwanda, without proper access to justice | Kerry Smith

entry.title: Washington Post: 14 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

  • Greed is dead. Long live greed!

  • No Republican candidate should be able to escape the Jan. 6 hearings
  • More guns in schools, and everywhere else? Have we gone mad?
  • How Ukraine’s ‘women-warriors’ are playing a vital role in the war
  • Has Justin Trudeau been ‘Americanized’ — or is he just left-wing?
  • I saw Mugabe wreck a democracy. The Jan. 6 hearings matter — a lot.
  • Fairfax County is a leader in promoting red-flag laws. Others should follow.
  • Is Virginia’s lieutenant governor paying attention to her friends?
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 14 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 14 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-14 18:24PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Historic Hong Kong mansion handed over to pro-Beijing think tank to transform into healthy living centre
  • February 8, 2016: Night of mob violence in Mong Kok leaves Hong Kong on edge
  • Beijing reveals blueprint for Nansha, with incentives that could draw young Hongkongers into calling the Guangzhou district home
  • Zuckerberg’s Meta to push metaverse initiatives to Hong Kong cafes and schools as interest in virtual world hots up
  • November 1, 2014: Expat banker Rurik Jutting arrested over murder of 2 women
  • HKMA sells US$560 million to defend Hong Kong currency peg from capital outflow ahead of expected US interest rate rise
  • October 1, 2012: Deadly ferry crash off Hong Kong’s Lamma Island kills 39
  • Cathay Pacific expecting better showing in first half of 2022 despite staying in the red, after strong cargo performance and cost-cutting moves
  • Hong Kong health officials change how they present daily number of Covid-19 infections
  • August 23, 2010: 7 Hong Kong tourists and guide killed in Manila bus hostage crisis
  • Hong Kong customs seizes phones, pharmaceuticals in haul worth HK$7.5 million hidden in excavators
  • July 1, 2003: 500,000 take to Hong Kong’s streets in protest against proposed national security legislation
  • Consumption vouchers: retailers, e-payment service providers offer promotions worth billions to entice Hongkongers ahead of roll-out
  • June 23, 2003: Hong Kong free of Sars, WHO declares
  • June 26, 1999: Beijing approves Hong Kong Basic Law reinterpretation on right of abode
  • August 14, 1998: ‘Billions spent to prop up shares’ - Hong Kong fights back amid financial crisis
  • Hong Kong’s iconic Jumbo Floating Restaurant leaves for new secret location as residents bid farewell
  • Hong Kong ‘will not give in a single inch’ to requests by business chambers, consulates to ease Covid rules, Lam says, citing state officials’ visit and transition of power
  • July 1, 1997: ‘The Handover’ - Hong Kong’s return to China after British rule
  • HKEX hoped alchemy would turn acquisition of century-old London Metal Exchange into ‘super-connector’ gold. What went wrong?
  • Global stocks slump to bear market for the first time since March 2020 as key MSCI index falls 21 per cent from its November record
  • July 22, 2009: Bank payouts on Lehman Brothers minibonds to top HK$6 billion
  • Hongkongers are living longer than ever, but experts flag health problems ahead
  • ‘If everybody criticises you, you probably got it right’: ex-chief justice Geoffrey Ma reflects on challenges for Hong Kong’s courts
  • Hong Kong’s uncomfortable silence on Queen Elizabeth’s platinum jubilee
  • Dozens of countries slam China at UN over Xinjiang abuse allegations
  • Where did water on the moon come from? Chinese scientists find clues in lunar samples
  • Apple CEO Tim Cook expects continued growth for ‘cutting-edge’ Chinese software developers amid rising geopolitical tensions
  • China’s consumption problem rooted in fears and uncertainties, while vouchers have limited appeal
  • Chinese concerns rise over US policy shift on Taiwan
  • China still backing Poland gateway plan despite impact of Ukraine war
  • Career diplomat Le Yucheng moves to Chinese state broadcasting body as deputy chief
  • Coronavirus: Taiwan’s economic outlook dims as Omicron outbreak casts a shadow over growth
  • Chinese AI giant Megvii’s chief scientist Sun Jian dies at age 45 of sudden illness
  • China says it has released Bloomberg staffer Haze Fan on bail
  • Omicron sub-variants latest challenge for China’s zero-Covid policy
  • China calls for overseas arrivals to be monitored for signs of monkeypox
  • Chinese smartphone brand Meizu sold to carmaker Geely as smart vehicles become latest frontier for Big Tech
  • Fears of data abuse as Chinese health code turns red for financial scandal protesters
  • Shanghai merchants, hard-hit by Covid-19 lockdown, delay reopening of Qipu Road clothing market, demand rent relief
  • ‘Absurd’: Taipei rejects Beijing’s sovereign claims over Taiwan Strait
  • China’s bid for Pacific Islands security pact should be considered in regional forum, says Samoan PM
  • Chinese police on anti-gang blitz after attack on women in Tangshan
  • Pixar’s Lightyear won’t show in some countries over same-sex kiss, with China screening also unlikely
  • US lawmakers reach compromise on screening investments in China
  • Two top US and China officials meet for a third time this year to ‘reduce risks’
  • China economy: with more US tightening on the horizon, could this be Beijing’s last chance to cut rates in 2022?
  • BTS announce temporary break, with plans for solo releases before reuniting as group
  • Are India’s billion-dollar trade ties with Gulf states in ‘peril’ amid Prophet Mohammed row?
  • Thai cat with Covid suspected to have infected human
  • Malaysia eases chicken export ban but not for Singapore’s favourite ‘broiler’
  • Take holiday to grow your own food, civil servants told by Sri Lankan government
  • AirAsia turns to India to fill gap left by absent China tourists due to Covid-19 travel curbs
  • Iko Uwais, Indonesian martial arts star who rose to fame in Hollywood and Netflix’s Wu Assassins accused of assaulting interior designer
  • India’s women, Muslims targeted by nationalist YouTube influencers, report finds
  • Cambodia jails ‘Lady Liberty’ lawyer for treason over Sam Rainsy’s failed return from exile, alongside dozens of others
  • Australia-China relations: Albanese says Beijing must lift sanctions on exports to reset ties
  • US defence chief denies seeking to split Asia ‘into hostile blocks’ during Thailand trip
  • Ukraine war fallout: Pakistan plunged into darkness as Europe gobbles up world’s LNG supplies
  • Marcos’ ‘confessions’: Philippines president-elect admits to ‘trolls’, needing guidance – and doing it for his parents
  • Jailed Putin critic Alexei Navalny abruptly moved to undisclosed location
  • Happy the elephant is not a person, top New York court rules – she is a ‘magnificent creature’
  • EU turns to Israel for gas after ‘blackmail’ by Russia over Ukraine war support
  • Pope says Russia’s ‘ferocious’ invasion perhaps provoked and we don’t see the ‘whole drama’
  • Ukraine war: ‘Surrender or die’ choice in Sievierodonetsk after Russian forces destroy bridges to city
  • Gruesome scenes and anguish in Ukraine frontline city Lysychansk: ‘They bomb and bomb’
  • ‘War good for business’ as drive to arm Ukraine looms over French expo
  • Ukraine exhumes bodies with ‘knees shot, hands tied’ from makeshift forest graves
  • UK sending first asylum seekers to Rwanda after London court refuses to halt deportation plan
  • EU to tackle deepfakes, with Big Tech like Google, Facebook, Twitter facing fines of 6 per cent of turnover
  • Congo military accuses Rwanda of invasion as rebels capture town
  • Kevin Spacey to appear in London court over sex offences charges
  • Google offers to let rival ad firms place ads on YouTube to address EU antitrust probe
  • Bear market hits Wall Street as stocks, bonds and cryptocurrencies dive
  • Donald Trump ‘detached from reality’ over election, top aides tell second Capitol riot hearing
  • WHO considers renaming monkeypox over fears of stigma and racism
  • As the industrial metaverse grows, the platform must remain open and available
  • Yuan currency most exposed to climate change – could lose half its value in 50 years, Barclays says
  • US senator questions whether HSBC was pressured to suspend banker over controversial climate-change comments
  • GoGoX defies IPO drought as it pushes through downsized Hong Kong offering targeting US$85.5 million, down from US$500 million
  • As China’s economy revs up, brace for even higher global energy prices and inflation
  • How rezoning of Hong Kong’s industrial sites for residential use could provide the city with homes it desperately needs
  • Most Hong Kong stocks weaken as global markets dive into bear market on Fed tightening overdrive, China lockdown concerns
  • Hong Kong fund managers’ lobby group urges incoming Chief Executive John Lee to drop quarantine rules
  • Has the Covid-19 pandemic finally pushed Japan towards flexible work arrangements?
  • Hong Kong stocks on track to trounce mainland peers as outlook for China’s tech giants brightens
  • Major Chinese cities see more land auctions being scrapped, as broke developers hobbled by ‘three red lines’ stay away
  • ByteDance’s Hong Kong entity registers multiple mainland subsidiaries in move seen as prelude to possible listing in the city
  • US bill to rein in Big Tech backed by dozens of small and big companies
  • Netflix K-drama review: Our Blues – Lee Byung-hun and Kim Hye-ja provide heart-rending ending for ensemble drama
  • Blood donation is easy and painless, says record donor in Hong Kong, who’s 70 and has given blood 190 times. How and why you should too
  • Japanese artist who was an ‘Instagram star’ years before the internet was born shows works in Hong Kong
  • The 15 best new Hollywood movies in cinemas this summer, from Elvis to Minions: The Rise of Gru and Thor: Love and Thunder
  • Man who knows where to find the best pizza in Hong Kong on his rating system, pies that surprised, and plans to meet his followers
  • When jury members in Hong Kong received death threat over manslaughter conviction for deadly knife and arson attack
  • How luxury handbag label Delvaux, the ‘Hermès of Belgium’, made an incredible comeback, with China the key
  • Lightyear movie review: Pixar’s Toy Story spin-off featuring Chris Evans’ voice is aimed squarely at children
  • Meet Riley Keough, Elvis Presley’s granddaughter – she modelled for Dior before acting and directing, and ‘loved’ stepdad Michael Jackson, but why didn’t she act in the Elvis biopic?
  • Who is Rachel Zegler, the new lead in Hunger Games’ prequel? She got her break in Steven Spielberg’s West Side Story, walks red carpets in Dior, and covers Lady Gaga on YouTube
  • Savoury desserts? Fine dining chefs are giving sweet treats a new take, from Michelin-starred Noma in Denmark’s dish with oyster stock, to Sawaan in Thailand’s crab claw-inspired concept
  • 5 K-drama stars with odd jobs before the fame: Kim Tae-ri worked in a supermarket while Kim Min-seok was a cook in a Japanese restaurant … what about So Ji-sub, Go Min-si and Pyo Ye-jin?
  • Why are waiting lists for luxury watches getting even longer? Collectors can queue up to 10 years for the most exclusive Rolex, Patek Phillipe or Audemars Piguet
  • Inside Tamannaah Bhatia’s luxury life: the millionaire Tollywood star lives in a US$2 million Mumbai complex, drives a BMW, and owns one of the world’s biggest diamonds
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
